LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFFS JANE FENG AND ZHEN ZHOU'S MOTION OF STAY IN SUPPORTING THE POINTS OF THE PLAINTIFFS IS DENIED. WHILE THE NATURE OF THE MOTION BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS AND THE RELIEF SOUGHT THEREBY IS UNCLEAR, THE MOTION ASSERTS NO VALID GROUNDS FOR RELIEF FROM THE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, WHICH WAS ISSUED AND ENTERED ON JANUARY 29, 2014 AND OF WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS SERVED WITH NOTICE ON JANUARY 30, 2014. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PERMIT PLAINTIFFS TO ALLEGE, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THE FACTS REGARDING THE LETTER DATED JANUARY 29, 2010 AND ITS DISCOVERY ON JUNE 2012 THAT MAY AFFECT THE TOLLING OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PER CCP 340.6. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT INCLUDE ANY ALLEGATIONS OF THE LETTER OR ITS DISCOVERY IN THEIR FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND THE COURT DECLINES TO CONSIDER THIS EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE IN RULING ON THE DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS GRANTED. (CCP 437C.) DEFENDANT YANG HAS MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF PERSUASION TO DEMONSTRATE THAT ALL CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 340.6(A). IN SEVERAL LETTERS DATED FROM OCTOBER 10, 2010 TO FEBRUARY 14, 2011, PLAINTIFFS ACCUSED DEFENDANT YANG OF MISHANDLING THEIR CASES AND VIOLATING THE RULES OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT. (YANG DECL., EXHIBIT A-4, A-6, A-8.) PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT MET THEIR SHIFTING BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS TOLLED BY THE LETTER DATED JANUARY 29, 2012 AND ITS DISCOVERY IN JUNE 2012. (PEREGRINE FUNDING, INC. V. SHEPPARD MULLIN RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP (2005) 133 CAL.APP.4TH 658, 685. SEE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, 6, 7.) IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT MR. CHEN WAS RETAINED BY PLAINTIFFS AND SENT THE AFOREMENTIONED LETTERS DEFENDANT YANG AND HIS COUNSEL. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT CLAIM IGNORANCE OF THE ACTIONS OF THEIR COUNSEL. DEFENDANT YANG'S OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR MAR-24-2014 VACATED. JUDGMENT FILED 1/29/14. (206)

Mtn Of Stay In Supporting The Points Of The Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JANE FENG AND ZHEN ZHOU, 415.876.6703, PLAINTIFFS IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
GEORGETTA BECK, 415.956.5400, FOR DEFENDANT DAVID YANG.

JANE FENG IS BEING ASSISTED BY MANDARIN-LANGUAGE INTERPRETER ZIAO CHEN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS JANE FENG AND ZHEN ZHOU'S Motion Of Stay In Supporting The Points Of The Plaintiffs is denied. While the nature of the motion brought by Plaintiffs and the relief sought thereby is unclear, the motion asserts no valid grounds for relief from the Order Granting Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment, which was issued and entered on January 29, 2014 and of which Plaintiff was served with notice on January 30, 2014. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JANE FENG & ZHEN ZHOOU, TEL. NO. 876-6703, PLAINTIFFS APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JERRY R. HAUSER OF MILLER HAUSER LLP, TEL. NO. 354-0131 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVID YANG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues IS Granted. (CCP 437c.) Defendant Yang has met his initial burden of persuasion to demonstrate that all claims are barred by the statute of limitations pursuant to CCP 340.6(a). In several letters dated from October 10, 2010 to February 14, 2011, Plaintiffs accused Defendant Yang of mishandling their cases and violating the Rules of Professional Conduct. (Yang Decl., Exhibit A-4, A-6, A-8.) Plaintiffs have not met their shifting burden to show that the statute of limitations is tolled by the letter dated January 29, 2012 and its discovery in June 2012. (Peregrine Funding, Inc. v. Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 658, 685. See Second Amended Complaint, 6, 7.) It is undisputed that Mr. Chen was retained by Plaintiffs and sent the aforementioned letters Defendant Yang and his counsel. Plaintiffs cannot claim ignorance of the actions of their counsel. Defendant Yang's objections are sustained. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JANE FENG, TEL. NO. 876-6703, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVID YANG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, to permit Plaintiffs to allege, if they can do so in good faith, the facts regarding the letter dated January 29, 2010 and its discovery on June 2012 that may affect the tolling of the statute of limitations per CCP 340.6. Plaintiffs did not include any allegations of the letter or its discovery in their First Amended Complaint, and the court declines to consider this extrinsic evidence in ruling on the demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR
